rulings.Case Title: MONZERRAT PRESTEGUI MENDOZA VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT,A GOVERNMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re: Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Plaintiff'S Guardian Ad Litem, Brigida Mendoza (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed Sep-17-2019)
rulings.Rulings: September 27, 2019 Line 6: Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The parties should be prepared to address whether Plaintiff seeks damages for the period between the prior guardian ad litem deposition and now. If not, the deposition appears unnecessary and does not provide sufficient basis to reopen discovery. CCP 2024.050(b). If yes, Defendant should be prepared to discuss why it waited until now to move to take the second deposition. Plaintiff should be prepared to discuss why it would be improper to order a limited reopening of discovery to permit a deposition that "would be entirely limited to [Plaintiff's] condition since her mother's previous deposition on 2/1/2017." (Hensley Ex. G). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/jpt).

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Seal Reply Evidence And Objections In Support Of Renewed Motions For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 27, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC Motion For Order To Seal Reply Evidence And Objections In Support Of Renewed Motions For Summary Judgment. Plaintiff Motiva Enterprises LLC's two motions for sealing orders are denied without prejudice. Defendant insurers were granted summary judgment against Motiva on August 26, 2019. Motiva has moved to seal essentially all of the "unredacted" summary judgment papers in their entirety, which would leave the public with access only to "redacted" versions of those papers. However, Motiva fails to make the showings that would allow the court to enter the express factual findings required. (CRC 2.550(d).) Two examples: CRC 2.550(d) requires showings of "an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record" and that the "proposed sealing is narrowly tailored." Labelling something "confidential" under a protective order does not relieve a party of its obligations under CRC 2.550 and 2.551 - a point Judge Kahn expressly made in the protective order when signing it. An earlier sealing request in May 2019 regarded a motion to take motions off calendar. In contrast, the current request regards wide-ranging motions for summary judgment that were granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(RU/302)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES POZZI VS. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 27, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues. (Part 1 of 2). Defendant Fire Insurance Exchange's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative summary adjudication is denied. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production. Plaintiff's complaint alleges that defendant "waived and/or are estopped and/or have forfeited any right to deny coverage." (Complaint, 11.) "[W]here an insurer reserves its right to claim non coverage under the policy, notice of the reservation must be given to the insured or the reservation is deemed waived." (Miller v. Elite Insurance Co. (1980) 100 Cal.App.3d 739, 753-754.) Defendant's motion, which relies solely on the pleadings in the underlying elder abuse case and the insurance policies, fails to address these issues. Generally, where "an affirmative defense contains an exception, a defendant must also negate the exception as part of its initial burden on summary judgment if, but only if, the complaint alleges facts triggering potential applicability of the exception." (Varshock v. Department of Forestry & Fire Protection (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 635, 651; see also Westlye v. Look Sports, Inc. (1993) 17 Cal.App.4th 1715, 1738, 22 Cal.Rptr.2d 781 [the initial burden only requires a defendant seeking summary judgment to address issues raised in the plaintiff's complaint].) Plaintiff may be able to establish the breach of contract cause of action. Defendant fails to show that the intentional acts exclusion of the homeowner's policy bars coverage. "In determining whether an insurer has a duty to defend under the terms of its policy, we look both to the allegations in the complaint and to the extrinsic facts known to the insurer that suggest that a claim might be covered. [citation]. The duty to defend exists when a third-party complaint can fairly be amended to state a covered liability based on the facts alleged, reasonably inferable, or otherwise known." (Federal Insurance Company v. Steadfast Insurance Company (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 668, 680.) In this case, the first amended complaint in the underlying action could fairly be amended to state a claim for negligence based on an accident or occurrence. For example, 46 accused plaintiff of "neglect" as defined in Welf. & Inst. Code § 15610.57 [defining neglect to include the negligent failure of providing care]. In fact, the second amended complaint did ultimately allege a negligence claim. Defendant also fails to address the vicarious liability allegations regarding the Seslars. (Federal Insurance, 209 Cal.App.4th at 681.) (Continued to Part 2). =(RU/302)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES POZZI VS. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Fire Insurance Exchange Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 27, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues. (Part 2 of 2). Defendant's reply argues that there is no coverage under the homeowner's policy's abuse exclusion. The moving papers only mention the "Molestation, Abuse or Corporal Punishment" exclusion in a footnote and there is no argument or adduced facts in the moving papers regarding how that exclusion applies in this case. The court concludes that the moving papers did not properly raise the issue regarding whether the abuse exclusion applies. Defendant fails to shift the burden on the bad faith cause of action and claim for punitive damages. There is a dispute of fact regarding whether defendant breached the homeowner's policy and the genuine dispute doctrine does not apply to third party duty to defend cases. (See Mt. Hawley Insurance Company v. Lopez (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1424 ["It is doubtful that the so-called 'genuine dispute doctrine' applies in third party duty to defend cases…"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling). =(RU/302)

rulings.Case Title: MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. COURTHOUSE VENTURES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Courthouse Ventures, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiff In Intervention'S Deposition Subpoenas For Business Records And For A Protecitve Order
rulings.Rulings: September 27, 2019 Line 3: Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for protective order is DENIED subject to the requirement that any production of records must be treated as confidential under an appropriate confidentiality stipulation or order. Esther Eames LLC articulates sufficient grounds to seek the records of CHV's transactions between MOAB and third parties relating to MOAB-owned properties to discover whether there have been improper overpayments. The financial records appear "directly relevant" to the claims and defenses related to these allegations. Look v. Penovatz (2019) 24 Cal.App.5th 61, 73 (party seeking discovery of an individual's personal financial information must show the information sought is directly relevant to a cause of action or defense such that disclosure is essential to fair resolution of the lawsuit). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/jpt).

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. MICHAEL E LIPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 27, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Michael Lipson's motion to quash is denied. Defendant recognized the court's jurisdiction by filing his motion to set aside default judgment, thus dispensing with the need for service of summons. (See Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) p. 3-51.) No sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant Michael Lipson's motion to set aside default judgment is granted under CCP §473(b). California's strong public policy favors deciding cases on their merits. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.) Defendant's alternate grounds need not be reached. Defendant shall file a responsive pleading by October 4, 2019. No sanctions are awarded. rulings.Case Title: ARMANDO REYES VS. STARBUCKS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 27, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION, JEN LU Motion To Reclassify Case. Defendants Starbucks Corporation and Jen Lu's motion to reclassify the case to limited jurisdiction is denied. As defendants concede, the test for their motion is whether damages in the jurisdictional amount are "virtually unattainable." (Ytuarte v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 266, 276-77.) Damages for pain and suffering are "not subject to precise pretrial measurement," so it is particularly difficult to win a reclassification motion in a case like this one. (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(RU/302)

rulings.Case Title: CRCM OPPORTUNITY FUND II, L.P. VS. ROAM INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: September 27, 2019 Line 4: The motion is GRANTED and DENIED in part. Boilerplate objections are inappropriate and "may be sanctionable." Korea Data Systems Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1516. Boilerplate objections that object "to the extent" a request seeks irrelevant or privileged documents are particularly problematic because it is not clear whether any documents are being withheld based on a claim of relevance or privilege. Any claim of privilege must be supported by a privilege log. CCP 2031.240(b)(2), (c)(1); Catalina Island Yacht Club v. Sup. Ct. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 116, 1130. Turning to the requests at issue. RFPs 1, 2, 3) Granted - Roam shall supplement its answers to state whether all responsive documents are produced and shall log any withheld based on a claim of privilege. RFP 4) Granted - the RFP is not vague or overbroad based on the referenced loan agreement and limitation to the closure of the property. RFPs 5, 6) Granted in part - the RFPs are overbroad and the Court limits them to all communications related to the closure of the properties. RFP 7, 8, 9, 10) Granted - the partial production is insufficient. RFP 13, 14, 21, 22) Granted - the RFPs are not vague or overbroad. Civil Code 3295 does not shield this information from discovery because it is not related to punitive damages but to the fundamental issues in dispute. Rawnsley v. Sup. Ct. (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 86, 91. RFPs 15, 16, 17) Granted - the RFPs are tailored to the leases at issue. SI 1, 9) Denied - while the Court disregards the general objections, the answer is otherwise responsive. SI 3, 4, 5, 6) Denied - while the Court disregards the general objections, and the answers are not fully compliant CCP 2030.230, that statute permits reference to documents. SI 7, 8) Granted - as discussed elsewhere, Civil Code 3295 does not shield this information. Further responses due by 10/18/2019. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $5,500 ($550/hour for 10 hours) to reflect partial success. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/jpt).
